Armaan Gomes, Intelligence

Experience

Education ​

Massachusetts Institute of TechnologyCambridge, MA — May 2029
B.S.E. Electrical Engineering with Computing · Minor in Materials Science/Engineering · GPA 5.0/5.0
  • Courses: Algorithms, Computational Structures, Low Level Programming
Homestead High SchoolCupertino, CA — June 2025
GPA 4.0/4.0
  • President: Programming Club, Cybersecurity Club
  • Science Bowl · Robotics Software Lead

Research & Work ​

MIT Ferroelectric Materials & Devices Group — Student ResearcherAug 2025–Present
  • Improving on-chip energy storage using ferroelectric material effects.
  • Tested wafers, analyzed, and optimized novel capacitors for energy and power density.
  • Evaluated plate and in-plane capacitor polarization, power, and breakdown properties.
UC Berkeley Nanotechnology Lab — Student ResearcherJun 2024–Aug 2025
  • Built low-cost 18-channel microfluidics potentiostat hardware.
  • Optimized Teensy-based potentiostat and ECG core for high data throughput.
  • Created FPGA firmware and C# driver for a 256-channel electrochemical camera.
BWSI: BASICs of ASICs, MIT Lincoln Labs — StudentJun 2024–Jul 2024
  • Learned ASIC fabrication, design, Verilog verification and simulation.
  • Led team of 6 to design and tape out SuperMic, an 8-channel beamforming ASIC.
  • Designed and fabricated 7 additional accelerator, beamforming, and specialized ASICs.

Activities ​

MIT Open Compute LaboratoryAug 2025–Present
  • Developed custom accelerators for DNNs; demonstrated PDM, Ternary, and INT8 inference.
  • Built a custom RV32-I CPU with pipelined design; compiled and ran Pong over VGA.
  • Designing a custom FPGA server cluster with 100 Gb/s+ inter-FPGA bandwidth and >1.6M logic slices.
MIT MotorsportsAug 2025–Present
  • Designing custom motor inverters for Formula SAE with 5× efficiency and 4× precision improvements.
  • Improving EMI, reliability, safety, and performance.

Awards ​

  • ISEF 2nd Award 2025 — $15k Chief of Naval Research Award
  • ISEF 3rd Award 2024 — Grand Award in Biomedical Engineering
  • MakeMIT 2nd Place — Entertainment Track
  • MIT Battlecode 2026 Finalist
  • CSEF First in Electronics (2024) · First in Computational Systems (2023)
  • Grand Prize — Best of Synopsys Championship (2024) · First Place (2023)
  • FRC Arizona East Regional Winner 2024
  • $1000 Medici Grant · PVSA Gold · picoCTF 2022 Top 2% · USACO Silver

Skills ​

Technical: ASIC Design · Verilog · Chisel · Yosys/OpenLane · C++ · Python · Java · C# · Altium · Vivado · Bash · CAD · Wafer Testing · Probe Manipulation

Hobbies: Taekwondo — Fourth Degree Black Belt, 12+ years
